function solve(nums) {
    const N = nums.length;

    const costs = new Array(P + 1);

        // Required value for nums[i] is neighbor_max + 1

        // Cost is max(0, required_value - current_value)

        costs[i] = cost;
    }

    let min_total_cost = Infinity;

    if (P % 2 !== 0) {
        // Case 1: P is odd (N is odd). There is only one Maximal Independent Set (MIS): {1, 3, 5, ..., P}

        // Indices: 1, 3, 5, ..., P
        for (let i = 1; i <= P; i += 2) {
            current_cost += costs[i];
        }
        min_total_cost = current_cost;
    } else {

        // Calculate Cost for S_odd

            cost_even += costs[i];
        }
        
        min_total_cost = Math.min(min_total_cost, cost_even);
    }

    return min_total_cost;
}

function solve(nums) {
    const N = nums.length;

        return 0;

    const costs = new Array(P + 1);

        // Required value for nums[i] is neighbor_max + 1
        const required_value = neighbor_max + 1;
        
        // Cost is max(0, required_value - current_value)
        const cost = Math.max(0, required_value - nums[i]);
        costs[i] = cost;
    }

    let min_total_cost = Infinity;

    if (P % 2 !== 0) {
        // Case 1: P is odd (N is odd). There is only one Maximal Independent Set (MIS): {1, 3, 5, ..., P}
        let current_cost = 0;
        // Indices: 1, 3, 5, ..., P
        for (let i = 1; i <= P; i += 2) {
            current_cost += costs[i];
        }
        min_total_cost = current_cost;
    } else {
        // Case 2: P is even (N is even). Two MISs: S_odd = {1, 3, ..., P-1} and S_even = {2, 4, ..., P}
        
        // Calculate Cost for S_odd
        let cost_odd = 0;
        // Indices: 1, 3, 5, ..., P-1
        for (let i = 1; i <= P - 1; i += 2) {
            cost_odd += costs[i];

        let cost_even = 0;
        // Indices: 2, 4, 6, ..., P
        for (let i = 2; i <= P; i += 2) {
            cost_even += costs[i];
        }
        
        min_total_cost = Math.min(min_total_cost, cost_even);
    }

    return min_total_cost;
}

Case 1: $N$ is odd. $P$ is odd. $P=2k+1$. $K=k+1$. There is only one set $S=\{1, 3, \dots, P\}$. We just calculate $C(S)$.
Case 2: $N$ is even. $P$ is even. $P=2k$. $K=k$. There are exactly two MISs: $S_{odd}=\{1, 3, \dots, P-1\}$ and $S_{even}=\{2, 4, \dots, P\}$. We calculate $C(S_{odd})$ and $C(S_{even})$, and take the minimum.

This structural property of maximal independent sets on path graphs saves us from the $O(N^2)$ DP!

### Implementation Plan

1. Calculate $P = N-2$.
2. Calculate $cost(i)$ for $i=1$ to $P$.
   $cost(i) = \max(0, \max(nums[i-1], nums[i+1]) + 1 - nums[i])$. (Remember array indexing: $nums[i]$ corresponds to the index $i$ in the problem statement, so $i$ runs from $1$ to $N-2$).
3. Determine the required sets $S$:
    a. If $N$ is odd ($P$ is odd): $S = \{1, 3, 5, \dots, P\}$. Calculate $\sum_{i \in S} cost(i)$.
    b. If $N$ is even ($P$ is even): $S_{odd} = \{1, 3, \dots, P-1\}$, $S_{even} = \{2, 4, \dots, P\}$. Calculate $C(S_{odd})$ and $C(S_{even})$. Return $\min(C(S_{odd}), C(S_{even}))$.

We need to handle the indices carefully. $N$ is the length of `nums`. Indices are $0, 1, \dots, N-1$. Special indices $i$ are $1, \dots, N-2$. $P=N-2$.

Let's trace Example 1: $nums = [1, 2, 2]$. $N=3$.
$P = 3-2 = 1$. $N$ is odd.
$i=1$. $cost(1) = \max(0, \max(nums[0], nums[2]) + 1 - nums[1])$
$cost(1) = \max(0, \max(1, 2) + 1 - 2) = \max(0, 2 + 1 - 2) = 1$.
$S = \{1\}$. Total cost = 1. Matches example.

Let's trace Example 2: $nums = [2, 1, 1, 3]$. $N=4$.
$P = 4-2 = 2$. $N$ is even.
Indices to check: $i=1, 2$. $K=1$.
$cost(1)$: $i=1$. Neighbors $nums[0]=2, nums[2]=1$. $nums[1]=1$.
$cost(1) = \max(0, \max(2, 1) + 1 - 1) = \max(0, 2 + 1 - 1) = 2$.
$cost(2)$: $i=2$. Neighbors $nums[1]=1, nums[3]=3$. $nums[2]=1$.
$cost(2) = \max(0, \max(1, 3) + 1 - 1) = \max(0, 3 + 1 - 1) = 3$.

$P=2$ is even. $K=1$.
$S_{odd} = \{1\}$. Cost = $cost(1) = 2$.
$S_{even} = \{2\}$. Cost = $cost(2) = 3$.
Minimum cost = 2. Matches example.

Let's trace Example 3: $nums = [5, 2, 1, 4, 3]$. $N=5$.
$P = 5-2 = 3$. $N$ is odd. $K=2$.
Indices to check: $i=1, 2, 3$.
$cost(1)$: $i=1$. Neighbors $nums[0]=5, nums[2]=1$. $nums[1]=2$.
$cost(1) = \max(0, \max(5, 1) + 1 - 2) = \max(0, 5 + 1 - 2) = 4$.
$cost(2)$: $i=2$. Neighbors $nums[1]=2, nums[3]=4$. $nums[2]=1$.
$cost(2) = \max(0, \max(2, 4) + 1 - 1) = \max(0, 4 + 1 - 1) = 4$.
$cost(3)$: $i=3$. Neighbors $nums[2]=1, nums[4]=3$. $nums[3]=4$.
$cost(3) = \max(0, \max(1, 3) + 1 - 4) = \max(0, 3 + 1 - 4) = 0$.

$P=3$ is odd. $S = \{1, 3\}$.
Total cost = $cost(1) + cost(3) = 4 + 0 = 4$. Matches example.

The structural analysis seems correct and leads to an $O(N)$ solution.

The costs involve large numbers ($10^9$), so we must use appropriate data types (standard JavaScript numbers are 64-bit floats, safe up to $2^{53} \approx 9 \cdot 10^{15}$, which is sufficient for sums of $10^5$ terms, each up to $10^9$).

Final check on complexity: $O(N)$ calculation of costs, $O(N)$ summation/comparison. Total $O(N)$. This is efficient enough.

Implementation details: Javascript function `solve(nums)`.

```javascript
function solve(nums) {
    const N = nums.length;
    if (N < 3) {
        return 0;
    }

    const P = N - 2; // Max index i we can choose, 1 to P

    // 1. Calculate costs for each potential peak index i (1 <= i <= P)
    // cost[i] stores the cost to make index i a peak. We use 1-based indexing conceptually, 
    // so we might use an array of size P+1.
    const costs = new Array(P + 1);

    for (let i = 1; i <= P; i++) {
        // Neighbors are i-1 and i+1
        const neighbor_max = Math.max(nums[i - 1], nums[i + 1]);
        
        // Required value for nums[i] is neighbor_max + 1
        const required_value = neighbor_max + 1;
        
        // Cost is max(0, required_value - current_value)
        const cost = Math.max(0, required_value - nums[i]);
        costs[i] = cost;
    }

    let min_total_cost = Infinity;

    if (P % 2 !== 0) {
        // Case 1: P is odd (N is odd). There is only one Maximal Independent Set (MIS): {1, 3, 5, ..., P}
        let current_cost = 0;
        // Indices: 1, 3, 5, ..., P
        for (let i = 1; i <= P; i += 2) {
            current_cost += costs[i];
        }
        min_total_cost = current_cost;
    } else {
        // Case 2: P is even (N is even). Two MISs: S_odd = {1, 3, ..., P-1} and S_even = {2, 4, ..., P}
        
        // Calculate Cost for S_odd
        let cost_odd = 0;
        // Indices: 1, 3, 5, ..., P-1
        for (let i = 1; i <= P - 1; i += 2) {
            cost_odd += costs[i];
        }
        min_total_cost = cost_odd;

        // Calculate Cost for S_even
        let cost_even = 0;
        // Indices: 2, 4, 6, ..., P
        for (let i = 2; i <= P; i += 2) {
            cost_even += costs[i];
        }
        
        min_total_cost = Math.min(min_total_cost, cost_even);
    }

    return min_total_cost;
}
